creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
A few days ago, I had never heard of this city, so I was quite surprised by how interesting Durham was 🙌  I went on a guided tour of the castle that dates back to 1072, and I thought about how wild it is that such an old castle is still in use as student accommodation 😅  Durham Cathedral is now on a shared 1st place on my ‘most beautiful cathedrals’ I ever been to (it shares the space with the St Vitus Cathedral in Prague) - worth a visit and especially if you’re a Harry Potter fan 😉  As mentioned I took the train from Newcastle, but you can also reach Durham from York in 45 minutes, from Leeds in about an hour or from London in just under 3 hours - all by train 🚄

I’m a massive sucker for these smaller British towns - where do you think I should go next? 🇬🇧   #durham #lovegreatbritain #traveltips

transcription.text: At this point I'm basically on a quest to visit every part of Britain, so today I got on a train from Newcastle and after about 10 minutes I ended up in this little university city called Durham. The city was founded over a thousand years ago by monks who sought a safe place to hide from the vikings, but as a Danish guy I hope that's all water under the bridge now. Durham Castle is now owned by Durham University, so in proper Hogwarts style it's home to over 100 students and the makers of the Harry Potter movies actually wanted to use their great hall as yeah the great hall. I honestly think that the cathedral which is right next to the castle is just as pretty as the one in Prague and I can only respect a place of worship that actually sells lager in their cafe and here they did film some of the scenes from the Harry Potter movies, so I was kind of expecting a lot more tourists, but I guess that this place is actually a bit of a hidden gem that I really think you should save and consider checking out.
